summary: No OWASP Top 10 concerns introduced by the Task 7 wiring: SAF URI is user-chosen and never handed cross-process, the suggested filename is built from SimpleDateFormat(Locale.ROOT) with no user input, Snackbar messages are string-resource constants (no PII / no exception-message leakage), the caught IOException is not re-logged via ErrorLog (Decision 10 respected), and crash.log is preserved on failure (crashState.clear() runs only after successful write). One info-level nit: saveLogAndClearCrash catches IOException only — a SecurityException or IllegalArgumentException from openOutputStream on a stale/revoked SAF URI would propagate out of Result and crash the coroutine; widening the catch is defensive-robustness, not a vulnerability.

findings.issue: saveLogAndClearCrash catches IOException only. ContentResolver.openOutputStream on a SAF URI can also throw SecurityException (stale/revoked URI grant after system state change) or IllegalArgumentException (malformed URI). On those, the exception escapes Result, bubbles up through coroutineScope.launch, and ends up on the Compose coroutine's default handler — the `finally { launching = false }` still runs, but the error Snackbar does not, and the user perceives the click as a silent no-op (or, depending on exception handler config, a silent process kill). Not a direct security vulnerability — the SAF URI is still user-controlled — but A09 (logging/monitoring) prefers that all failure modes route through the same Snackbar path so the user sees a consistent error signal.
